title: The Marian Concise Anthology, One Quote per Saint, with Greek and Latin

saints.name: St. Justin Martyr
saints.era_title: Part I — Apostolic and Sub-Apostolic Era
saints.original: Εὕα γὰρ παρθένος καὶ ἄφθορος... ἀκούσασα δὲ τὸν λόγον τὸν ἀπὸ τοῦ ὄφεως ἔτεκε παρακοὴν καὶ θάνατον· πίστιν δὲ καὶ χαρὰν λαβοῦσα Μαρία ἡ παρθένος... ἀπεκρίνατο· γένοιτό μοι κατὰ τὸ ῥῆμά σου.
saints.english: Eve, a virgin and undefiled, having conceived the word of the serpent, brought forth disobedience and death. But the Virgin Mary, receiving faith and joy when the angel Gabriel announced to her the good tidings, answered: Be it done unto me according to thy word.
saints.source: Dialogue with Trypho, ch. 100.

saints.name: St. Irenaeus of Lyons
saints.era_title: Part I — Apostolic and Sub-Apostolic Era
saints.original: Sicut illa per angeli sermonem seducta est ut effugeret Deum praevaricata verbum eius, ita et haec per angelicum sermonem evangelizata est ut portaret Deum obediens eius verbo. Et si ea inobedierat Deo, sed haec suasa est obedire Deo, uti virginis Evae virgo Maria fieret advocata.
saints.english: Just as that one [Eve] was seduced by the word of an angel to flee from God, transgressing His word, so this one [Mary] was evangelized by the word of an angel that she might bear God, obeying His word. And while the former had disobeyed God, the latter was persuaded to obey God, that the Virgin Mary might become the advocate [advocata] of the virgin Eve.
saints.source: Adversus Haereses V.19.1.

saints.name: Tertullian of Carthage
saints.era_title: Part I — Apostolic and Sub-Apostolic Era
saints.original: Eva crediderat serpenti, Maria credidit Gabrieli; quod illa credendo deliquit, ista credendo delevit.
saints.english: Eve believed the serpent; Mary believed Gabriel. What the one destroyed by believing, the other erased by believing.
saints.source: De Carne Christi 17.

saints.name: Sub Tuum Praesidium — Greek, late 3rd century
saints.era_title: Part I — Apostolic and Sub-Apostolic Era
saints.original: Ὑπὸ τὴν σὴν εὐσπλαγχνίαν καταφεύγομεν, Θεοτόκε. Τὰς ἡμῶν ἱκεσίας μὴ παρίδῃς ἐν περιστάσει, ἀλλ᾽ ἐκ κινδύνων λύτρωσαι ἡμᾶς, μόνη ἁγνή, μόνη εὐλογημένη.
saints.english: Beneath thy compassion we take refuge, O Mother of God: do not despise our petitions in time of trouble, but rescue us from dangers, only pure, only blessed one.
saints.source: P. Ryl. III 470, the oldest known Marian prayer.

saints.name: Origen of Alexandria
saints.english: (Commentary on John I.6, fragment): "No one can grasp the meaning of the Gospel of John except he who has reclined upon the breast of Jesus, and who has received from Jesus Mary to be his own mother.
saints.source: Commentary on John, Preface, §6.

saints.name: St. Ephrem the Syrian
saints.english: (Hymns on the Nativity 16.10, Syriac original in CSCO 186): "Just as bodies were formed from Adam without the agency of a woman, so the body of the Second Adam was formed from a virgin without the agency of a man... O only woman whose praise is great in heaven and on earth! Through thee the world has been raised; through thee the angels rejoice." English (Oratio ad Sanctissimam Dei Matrem, traditionally attributed): "After the Mediator, thou art the Mediatress of the whole world.
saints.source: Hymns on the Nativity 16 (verbatim); Oratio (traditional

saints.name: St. Athanasius
saints.english: (Athanasian school, Homily of the Papyrus of Turin): "O noble Virgin, truly thou art greater than any other greatness. For who is thy equal in greatness, O dwelling place of God the Word? To whom among all creatures shall I compare thee, O Virgin?
saints.source: Homily on the Papyrus of Turin; Athanasian school.

saints.name: St. Cyril of Jerusalem
saints.original: Διὰ τῆς ἁγίας Παρθένου Μαρίας πάντα τὰ ἀγαθὰ ἡμῖν προσῆλθεν.
saints.english: Through the holy Virgin Mary all good things have come to us.
saints.source: Catechetical Lectures 12.15.

saints.name: St. Gregory of Nazianzus
saints.original: Εἴ τις οὐ Θεοτόκον τὴν ἁγίαν Μαρίαν ὑπολαμβάνει, χωρὶς ἐστι τῆς θεότητος.
saints.english: If anyone does not believe that Holy Mary is the Mother of God, he is severed from the Godhead.
saints.source: Letter 101 (First Letter to Cledonius), PG 37, 177c.

saints.name: St. John Chrysostom
saints.english: (Homily on the Annunciation, attrib.): "What shall I say, what shall I speak? I am amazed at the wonder. Adam, formed of virgin earth, becomes the beginning of the human race; Mary, the virgin earth, becomes the beginning of human salvation.
saints.source: Homily on the Annunciation, PG 50, 791ff. (the

saints.name: St. Cyril of Alexandria
saints.original: Χαῖρε ἀπὸ ἡμῶν, Μαρία Θεοτόκε, σεμνὸν κειμήλιον ἁπάσης τῆς οἰκουμένης... δι᾽ ἧς Τριὰς δοξάζεται καὶ προσκυνεῖται, δι᾽ ἧς ὁ τίμιος σταυρὸς ὑμνεῖται καὶ προσκυνεῖται ἐν τῇ οἰκουμένῃ, δι᾽ ἧς οὐρανὸς ἀγάλλεται, δι᾽ ἧς ἄγγελοι καὶ ἀρχάγγελοι εὐφραίνονται, δι᾽ ἧς δαίμονες φυγαδεύονται, δι᾽ ἧς ὁ πεσὼν ἄνθρωπος εἰς οὐρανὸν ἀναλαμβάνεται... δι᾽ ἧς ψυχαὶ σώζονται.
saints.english: Hail Mary, Mother of God, venerable treasure of the whole universe... through whom the Trinity is glorified and adored, through whom the precious cross is hymned and venerated throughout the world, through whom heaven rejoices, through whom angels and archangels are filled with joy, through whom devils are put to flight, through whom fallen man is taken up into heaven... through whom souls are saved.
saints.source: Homily 4 delivered at the Council of Ephesus, 431.

saints.name: St. Peter Chrysologus
saints.original: Virgo concepit, virgo peperit, virgo permansit. Salus venit per feminam, sicut mors venit per feminam.
saints.english: A virgin conceived, a virgin gave birth, a virgin remained. Salvation came through a woman, just as death came through a woman.

saints.name: St. Ambrose of Milan
saints.original: Maria templum Dei, non Deus templi. Et ideo illa sola adoranda, qui in suo templo operabatur.
saints.english: Mary is the temple of God, not the God of the temple. And therefore He alone is to be adored who was working in His temple.
saints.source: De Institutione Virginis 49.

saints.name: St. Jerome
saints.original: Mors per Hevam, vita per Mariam.
saints.english: Death through Eve; life through Mary.
saints.source: Letter 22 to Eustochium, §21.

saints.name: St. Augustine of Hippo
saints.original: ...quia cooperata est caritate, ut fideles in Ecclesia nascerentur, quae illius capitis membra sunt.
saints.english: ...because she cooperated by love that the faithful might be born in the Church, who are members of that Head.
saints.source: De Sancta Virginitate §6.

saints.name: The Akathist Hymn
saints.original: Χαῖρε, κλίμαξ ἐπουράνιε, δι᾽ ἧς κατέβη ὁ Θεός· χαῖρε, γέφυρα μετάγουσα τοὺς ἐκ γῆς πρὸς οὐρανόν· χαῖρε, λύτρωσις τῶν δακρύων τῆς Εὔας.
saints.english: Rejoice, heavenly ladder by which God descended; rejoice, bridge leading those of earth to heaven; rejoice, redemption of the tears of Eve.
saints.source: Akathist Hymn, Stanza 2.

saints.name: St. Sophronius of Jerusalem
saints.english: (Sermo in Annuntiationem, PG 87, 3, 3217ff.): "No one is filled with the knowledge of God except through thee, O most holy one; no one is saved except through thee, O Mother of God; no one is ransomed from danger except through thee, O Virgin Mother.
saints.source: Marian homilies of the patriarch of Jerusalem.

saints.name: St. Germanus of Constantinople
saints.english: (Homily on the Sash of the Theotokos, PG 98): "No one obtains the gift of mercy save through thee, O most pure; no one is saved save through thee, O most holy; no one is delivered from evils save through thee, O most chaste; no one obtains a gift of grace save through thee, O all-immaculate.
saints.source: Sermons on the Mother of God, PG 98.

saints.name: St. Andrew of Crete
saints.english: (Homily on the Dormition II): "Today the source of life is laid in the tomb, and through her own death she becomes a ladder by which God comes down to mortals... Hail, full of grace; hail, by whom is restored mourning Eve; hail, Mediatress [Mesitria] of the world.
saints.source: Homiliae, PG 97.

saints.name: St. John of Damascus
saints.original: Σὺ θαῦμα τῶν θαυμάτων, ἡ σώζουσα τὸν κόσμον μεσῖτις.
saints.english: Thou art the wonder of all wonders, the saving Mediatress of the world.
saints.source: Homilies on the Dormition I-III, PG 96.

saints.name: St. Peter Damian
saints.original: Tibi nihil impossibile, cui possibile est etiam desperatos in spem salutis revocare.
saints.english: Nothing is impossible for thee, who canst even raise those who are in despair to the hope of salvation.
saints.source: Sermons on the Nativity of Mary.

saints.name: St. Anselm of Canterbury
saints.original: Nihil Mariae aequale, nihil nisi Deus maius Maria.
saints.english: Nothing equals Mary; nothing but God is greater than Mary.
saints.source: Oratio 52, Prayer to the Virgin.

saints.name: St. Bernard of Clairvaux
saints.original: Totum nos habere voluit per Mariam.
saints.english: He willed that we should have everything through Mary.
saints.source: Sermon on the Aqueduct (In Nativitate B. Mariae), §7.

saints.name: St. Albert the Great
saints.original: Beata Virgo electa est a Deo non in ministram alicuius particularis, sed in cooperatricem et adiutricem totius eius negotii.
saints.english: The Blessed Virgin was chosen by God not to be the minister of some particular work, but to be the cooperatrix and helper [cooperatrix et adiutrix] of His whole plan.
saints.source: Mariale super Missus est, q. 42 (Albertine school).

saints.name: St. Bonaventure
saints.original: Sicut luna inter solem et terram interposita, quod a sole accipit, terrae communicat, sic Maria inter Christum et nos posita, gratias quas a Christo accipit, nobis effundit.
saints.english: As the moon, set between the sun and the earth, communicates to the earth what it receives from the sun, so Mary, set between Christ and us, pours out upon us the graces she receives from Christ.
saints.source: Speculum B.V.M. ch. 6 (Bonaventurian school).

saints.name: St. Thomas Aquinas
saints.original: ...expectabatur consensus Virginis loco totius humanae naturae.
saints.english: ...the consent of the Virgin in the place of the whole human nature was awaited [at the Annunciation].
saints.source: Summa Theologiae III, q. 30, a. 1, c.

saints.name: St. Bridget of Sweden
saints.original: Mater mea et ego salvavimus hominem quasi uno corde tantum, ego patiendo in corde et carne, illa dolore et amore cordis sui.
saints.english: My Mother and I have saved man as it were with one Heart only: I by suffering in My Heart and flesh, she by the sorrow and love of her heart.
saints.source: Revelations I.35 (private revelation; received in the

saints.name: Jean Gerson
saints.era_title: Part VI — Late Medieval and Renaissance
saints.original: Maria coredemptrix nostra.
saints.english: Mary, our Co-Redemptrix.
saints.source: Marian writings (the precise textual provenance of

saints.name: St. Bernardine of Siena
saints.era_title: Part VI — Late Medieval and Renaissance
saints.original: Omnis gratia quae communicatur huic mundo triplicem habet processum: nam ordinatissime a Deo in Christum, a Christo in Virginem, a Virgine in nos dispensatur.
saints.english: Every grace communicated to this world has a threefold motion: for, by a most ordered dispensation, it is given from God to Christ, from Christ to the Virgin, from the Virgin to us.
saints.source: Sermo 51, in Opera Omnia, Quaracchi, vol. II.

saints.name: St. Peter Canisius
saints.era_title: Part VII — Counter-Reformation and Early Modern
saints.original: Beatissima Virgo cum Christo Redemptore nostro adeo coniuncta est, ut in opere redemptionis verissime cooperatrix sit dicenda.
saints.english: The Most Blessed Virgin is so united with Christ our Redeemer that she must most truly be called a cooperatrix in the work of redemption.
saints.source: *De Maria Virgine Incomparabili et Dei Genitrice

saints.name: St. Robert Bellarmine
saints.era_title: Part VII — Counter-Reformation and Early Modern
saints.original: Maria... Filium suum in sacrificium obtulit, tantis doloribus cum eo afflicta, quantis nulla unquam creatura.
saints.english: Mary offered her Son in sacrifice, afflicted with such sufferings together with Him as no other creature ever bore.
saints.source: De Septem Verbis Christi in Cruce, Book I, ch. 12.

saints.name: St. Francis de Sales
saints.era_title: Part VII — Counter-Reformation and Early Modern
saints.original: Marie est Médiatrice d'intercession entre nous et Jésus-Christ, comme Jésus-Christ est Médiateur de rédemption entre nous et Dieu son Père.
saints.english: Mary is Mediatrix of intercession between us and Jesus Christ, just as Jesus Christ is Mediator of redemption between us and God His Father.
saints.source: Treatise on the Love of God III.8 (substance); Letters.

saints.name: St. John Eudes
saints.era_title: Part VII — Counter-Reformation and Early Modern
saints.english: (The Admirable Heart of Mary, Book III): "The Heart of Mary is so completely united to the Heart of Jesus, and so transformed into it, that they are no longer two hearts but one single Heart.
saints.source: Le Cœur Admirable de la Très Sacrée Mère de Dieu, Book III.

saints.name: St. Louis Marie de Montfort
saints.era_title: Part VII — Counter-Reformation and Early Modern
saints.original: Dieu le Père a fait un amas de toutes les eaux qu'il a appelé la mer (*maria*) ; il a fait un amas de toutes ses grâces qu'il a appelé **Marie**.
saints.english: God the Father gathered all the waters together and called them the seas (maria); He gathered all His graces together and called them Mary (Maria).
saints.source: True Devotion to Mary, §23.

saints.name: St. Alphonsus Liguori
saints.era_title: Part VII — Counter-Reformation and Early Modern
saints.original: Tutte le grazie ci vengono dispensate per mezzo di Maria, e tutti quelli che si salvano si salvano solo per mezzo di questa divina Madre.
saints.english: All graces are dispensed to us through Mary, and all those who are saved are saved only by means of this Divine Mother.
saints.source: Le Glorie di Maria, Part I, ch. 5.

saints.name: St. Catherine Labouré
saints.original: Ô Marie, conçue sans péché, priez pour nous qui avons recours à vous.
saints.english: O Mary, conceived without sin, pray for us who have recourse to thee.
saints.source: Apparition at Rue du Bac, 27 November 1830.

saints.name: St. Bernadette Soubirous
saints.original: Que soy era Immaculada Councepciou.
saints.english: I am the Immaculate Conception.
saints.source: Sixteenth apparition at Lourdes, on the feast of the

saints.name: St. Maximilian Maria Kolbe
saints.original: Niepokalana jest Pośredniczką wszelkich łask.
saints.english: The Immaculata is the Mediatrix of all graces.
saints.source: Pisma (Writings), conferences, and the Militia Immaculatae

saints.name: St. (Padre) Pio of Pietrelcina
saints.original: Amate la Madonna e fatela amare. Recitate sempre il Rosario.
saints.english: Love the Madonna and make her loved. Always pray the Rosary.
saints.source: Epistolario and pastoral counsel, passim.

saints.name: Bl. Fulton J. Sheen
saints.original: When a woman gives birth, she does so with much pain; Mary in giving birth at Bethlehem had no pain; she had her pain at Calvary, when the Mystical Body of her Son was born.
saints.source: The World's First Love, ch. 12.

saints.name: St. Teresa of Calcutta
saints.original: Mary, Mother of Jesus, please be a mother to me now.
saints.source: Mother Teresa: In My Own Words and conferences.

saints.name: Bl. Pope Pius IX
saints.original: Ipsa enim eximio prorsus modo venenosi serpentis caput contrivit, et salutem mundo attulit.
saints.english: She, in her wholly singular way, crushed the poisonous head of the serpent and brought salvation to the world.
saints.source: Ineffabilis Deus, apostolic constitution defining the

saints.name: Pope Leo XIII
saints.original: ...Maria... omnis gratiae et doni canalis.
saints.english: Mary... is the channel of every grace and gift.
saints.source: Fidentem Piumque Animum (1896).

saints.name: Pope St. Pius X
saints.original: ...merito ipsam reparatricem perditi orbis et dispensatricem omnium munerum, quae nobis Iesus nece et sanguine comparavit, appellare possumus.
saints.english: We may with truth and propriety call her the Reparatrix of the lost world, and therefore the Dispensatrix of all the gifts that Jesus has acquired for us by His death and by His Blood.
saints.source: Ad Diem Illum Laetissimum (1904).

saints.name: Pope Benedict XV
saints.original: Ita cum Filio patiente et moriente passa est et paene commortua; sic materna in Filium iura pro hominum salute abdicavit, placandaeque Dei iustitiae, quantum ad se pertinebat, Filium immolavit, ut merito dici queat eam **cum Christo humanum genus redemisse**.
saints.english: To such an extent did she suffer and almost die with her suffering and dying Son; to such an extent did she surrender her maternal rights over her Son for man's salvation, and immolated Him, insofar as she could, to appease the justice of God, that we may rightly say she redeemed the human race together with Christ.
saints.source: Inter Sodalicia (1918), AAS 10:181-182.

saints.name: Pope Pius XI
saints.original: O Madre di amore e di misericordia, che quando il tuo dolcissimo Figlio consumava la Redenzione del genere umano sull'ara della croce, sei stata a Lui vicina, soffrendo con Lui come Corredentrice...
saints.english: O Mother of love and mercy, who, when thy sweetest Son was consummating the Redemption of the human race on the altar of the Cross, didst stand next to Him, suffering with Him as Coredemptrix...
saints.source: Radio message to Lourdes, 28 April 1935. AAS 27 (1935) 158.

saints.name: Ven. Pope Pius XII
saints.original: Ipsa quae, ab omni labe, sive personali, sive hereditaria, immunis et arctissime semper cum Filio suo coniuncta, eumdem in Golgotha, una cum maternorum iurium maternique amoris holocausto, nova veluti Eva, pro omnibus Adae filiis... aeterno Patri obtulit.
saints.english: She it was who, immune from all sin, personal or inherited, and ever most closely united with her Son, offered Him on Golgotha to the Eternal Father, together with the holocaust of her maternal rights and motherly love, like a new Eve, for all the children of Adam.
saints.source: Mystici Corporis Christi (1943).

saints.name: Second Vatican Council, Lumen Gentium §62
saints.original: Quapropter Beata Virgo in Ecclesia titulis Advocatae, Auxiliatricis, Adiutricis, **Mediatricis** invocatur. Quod tamen ita intelligitur, ut dignitati et efficacitati Christi unius Mediatoris nihil deroget, nihil superaddat.
saints.english: Therefore the Blessed Virgin is invoked in the Church under the titles of Advocate, Helper, Benefactress, and Mediatrix. This, however, is so understood that it neither takes away anything from nor adds anything to the dignity and efficacy of Christ the one Mediator.
saints.source: Lumen Gentium ch. VIII, §62.

saints.name: Pope St. John Paul II
saints.original: Maria, sebbene concepita e nata immune dalla colpa originale, ha partecipato in maniera mirabile alle sofferenze del suo Figlio divino, per essere **Corredentrice** dell'umanità.
saints.english: Mary, though conceived and born without the taint of original sin, participated in a marvelous way in the sufferings of her divine Son, in order to be Coredemptrix of humanity.
saints.source: General Audience of 8 September 1982; the title

saints.name: St. Epiphanius of Salamis
saints.original: Ἐν τιμῇ Μαρία, ὁ Κύριος προσκυνείσθω.
saints.english: Let Mary be held in honour; the Lord alone be adored.
saints.source: Panarion (Refutation of All Heresies) 79, against the

saints.name: St. Bede the Venerable
saints.original: Beata Maria... non solum credendo intra Patris arcanum sacramenta cognovit, sed in carne suscepit Verbum, et cum carne, ut ait Apostolus, plenitudinem deitatis.
saints.english: Blessed Mary, by believing, not only entered into the hidden mystery of the Father, but received the Word in her flesh and, with that flesh, the fullness of the Godhead.
saints.source: Homiliae Evangelii I.4, on the Visitation (PL 94, 12;

saints.name: Bl. Amadeus of Lausanne
saints.original: Aurora consurgens, electa ut sol, pulchra ut luna, terribilis ut castrorum acies ordinata.
saints.english: Rising dawn, chosen as the sun, beautiful as the moon, terrible as an army set in battle array.
saints.source: Eight Homilies on the Praise of Mary (Sources

saints.name: Bl. John Duns Scotus
saints.original: Potuit, decuit, ergo fecit.
saints.english: He could; it was fitting; therefore he did.
saints.source: Ordinatio III, d.3, q.1, Vatican Critical Edition vol.

saints.name: St. Lawrence Justinian
saints.english: (Sermo de Annuntiatione, 15th-century Patriarch of Venice; substance): "The Holy Spirit, like a great river, flowed into the Heart of Mary, and from the Heart of Mary flows back into us. She is the channel by which graces are dispensed, the maternal aqueduct of the divine mercy.
saints.source: Sermo de Annuntiatione and *De Casto Connubio Verbi

saints.name: St. Teresa of Ávila
saints.original: Tomé por mi señora y abogada a la gloriosa Virgen, y me encomendé a ella muy de veras.
saints.english: I took the glorious Virgin for my Lady and Advocate and entrusted myself wholly to her.
saints.source: Libro de la Vida (Autobiography), ch. 1, on her

saints.name: Bl. John Henry Newman
saints.english: (Letter to Pusey on Occasion of His Eirenicon, 1866; the famous Marian passage): "Mary is the daughter of Sion exalted, the lady to whom all the prophets minister, who is, after the Eternal Word, the most exalted of beings.
saints.source: Letter to Dr. Pusey on Occasion of His Eirenicon
